title: agraulus flavescens
intro: Agraulus flavescens, also known as the golden agalloch, is a type of tree found in tropical regions of Asia. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Agraulus flavescens include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Agraulus flavescens include the bark being made into a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
